name: Reconquista Trail: Sotres to Áliva Refuge
name_original: Sotres - Refugio de Áliva – Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa
region: Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa
description: Mountain bike a moderate 12.3-mile (19.8 km) route from Sotres to Refugio de Áliva, exploring the high-mountain landscapes of Picos de Europ
region_intro: Mountain biking around Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa offers diverse terrain across its dramatic limestone karst topography, deep gorges, and alpine meadows. The region, spanning Asturias, Cantabria, and Castile y León, features sharp peaks, lush forests, and river valleys. Riders can navigate twisting forest singletrack, challenging high passes, and open meadows, with significant elevation changes throughout the area. The landscape is shaped by ancient glacial activity, creating a rugged environment suitable for varied mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa The most popular mountain bike trail is Ándara Massif Trail, a 14.8 miles (24 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the rugged Ándara Massif, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Portillo del Boqueron – circular desde Fuente Dé por el Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa, a difficult 35.4 miles (57 km) path. This extensive circular route explores the park's varied landscapes, including significant climbs and descents. Local mountain bikers also love the Reconquista Trail: Sotres to Áliva Refuge, a 12.3 miles (20 km) trail leading through mountain passes and high-altitude terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa is defined by dramatic limestone peaks, deep river gorges, and lush beech and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa?
A: There are 13 mountain bike trails documented in Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with significant elevation gains across the dramatic limestone peaks and deep gorges of the region.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Picos de Europa?
A: While many routes in Picos de Europa are challenging, there are options for different fitness levels. Currently, komoot lists 3 moderate mountain bike trails. For families or beginners, it's advisable to check the difficulty ratings and elevation profiles carefully. The region's rugged terrain often means even moderate routes can have demanding sections.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Picos de Europa?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Picos de Europa is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is more stable, and trails are less likely to be affected by snow or heavy rain, especially at higher altitudes. Always check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out, as mountain weather can change rapidly.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Picos de Europa?
A: Mountain biking in Picos de Europa offers incredibly varied terrain. You can expect twisting forest singletrack, challenging high passes, and open alpine meadows. The rugged landscape, shaped by dramatic limestone karst topography, includes rock gardens and wallrides on some trails, providing technical challenges. You'll navigate deep gorges, lush forests, and serene alpine meadows, often with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Picos de Europa?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169193614/portillo-del-boqueron-circular-desde-fuente-de-por-el-parque-nacional-de-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo del Boqueron – circular desde Fuente Dé por el Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 57 km path that explores varied landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170755781/macizo-del-cornion-circular-por-posade-de-valdeon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Macizo del Cornión – circular por Posade de Valdeón&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 29 km route.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in Picos de Europa?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the Picos de Europa offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169410831/andara-massif-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ándara Massif Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24 km route traversing rugged terrain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1174309885/puerto-del-ponton-and-puerto-de-panderrueda-loop-from-oseja-de-sajambre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Pontón and Puerto de Panderrueda Loop from Oseja de Sajambre&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 22.8 km with significant climbs.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Picos de Europa?
A: The trails in Picos de Europa offer breathtaking scenery and access to unique natural features. You can encounter dramatic limestone peaks, deep river gorges like the Cares Gorge, and lush beech and oak forests. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/971417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Cares&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/280873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cable Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and various mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panderrueda Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3444006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Pandébano&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views.

Q: Are there any facilities or viewpoints accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, some facilities and viewpoints are accessible. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Dé Cable Car&lt;/a&gt; is a significant facility that can transport you to higher elevations, opening up more riding possibilities. Viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/280873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cable Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4643710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pandetrave Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning panoramic vistas of the dramatic mountain landscape.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about riding in Picos de Europa?
A: The mountain biking experience in Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders consistently praise the stunning views, the diverse and challenging terrain, and the opportunity to immerse themselves in a rich natural environment with unique geological formations like the limestone karst topography and deep gorges.

Q: What should I know about wildlife when mountain biking in Picos de Europa?
A: Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, home to diverse ecosystems and wildlife. While enjoying the trails, you might encounter various species, including chamois and raptors. The park is also home to endangered species like the Cantabrian brown bear and Iberian wolf, though sightings are rare. It's important to respect wildlife, stay on marked trails, and avoid disturbing their habitats. For more information on the park's conservation efforts, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.unesco.org/en/mab/picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;UNESCO Biosphere Reserve page&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take in Picos de Europa?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Picos de Europa varies significantly depending on the route's length, difficulty, and your fitness level. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169410831/andara-massif-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ándara Massif Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 24 km difficult route, typically takes about 2 hours 45 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169193614/portillo-del-boqueron-circular-desde-fuente-de-por-el-parque-nacional-de-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo del Boqueron circular&lt;/a&gt; (57 km) can take over 6 hours. Always check the estimated duration for each specific route on komoot.
tips: The Invernales del Texu, which belong to Sotres, appear on the path at the beginning of the route that leads to the Urriellu refuge, just below the track that goes up to Áliva. A charming semi-abandoned town encased in the valley.

From the Aliva Refuge, we descend with the mtb along the entire Llomba del Toro, which is actually a large glacial moraine that you run along the ridge, until we reach Las Vegas del Toro at the foot of the Las Moñetas Valley. At this point we continue parallel to the River Duje, on quite flat terrain, until the Invernales del Texu.

Family-run, the Áliva refuge is located in a wonderful place surrounded by the landscape of the Picos de Europa. It works as a hotel (70 seats) and you can also order some food if you feel like it, the attention is very cordial and the place is unique.
From here all the paths and tracks through the park are restricted for bicycles.
More information on the web:
https://cantur.com/instalaciones/7-hotel-aliva

faqs.question: Where does the mountain bike trail from Sotres to Refugio de Áliva start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts in Sotres. While specific parking details aren't provided, Sotres is a small village, and you can usually find parking in or near the village center to begin your ride. From there, the route heads towards the Áliva mountain refuge hotel.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of gravel (about 17.2 km) and paths (around 17.5 km). You'll also encounter smaller sections of asphalt, unpaved tracks, and singletrack. Be prepared for natural features like the Llomba del Toro, a large glacial moraine, and relatively flat sections parallel to the River Duje. Some parts may require you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to spectacular views of the Picos de Europa National Park, including high-mountain vistas and green meadows. The route passes through or near charming spots like Invernales del Texu and Vegas del Toro. You'll also see the Hermitage of the Virgen de las Nieves, old lead and zinc mines, and the stunning setting of the Áliva mountain refuge hotel.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginner riders?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While some sections might be manageable, it generally requires good fitness, and advanced riding skills might be necessary for certain parts. You should be prepared to dismount and push your bike on some portions, making it less ideal for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Sotres - Refugio de Áliva route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike in the Picos de Europa National Park, including this route, is typically during the warmer, drier months, generally from late spring through early autumn (May to October). This avoids snow and ice, and offers more stable weather conditions for high-mountain riding.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail in Picos de Europa National Park dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Picos de Europa National Park generally allows dogs, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and livestock. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you carry out any waste.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in Picos de Europa National Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking this route within the Picos de Europa National Park. However, it's essential to respect all national park regulations, especially regarding trail use, waste disposal, and wildlife protection.

faqs.question: Can I find food or accommodation along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant landmark along the trail is the Áliva mountain refuge hotel. It provides a welcoming stop for accommodation and food amidst the dramatic landscape, making it a great place to refuel or even stay overnight.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route connect with other well-known trails in Picos de Europa?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other notable trails. Significant portions of the route overlap with sections of the El Anillo de Picos (3 Macizos) and the Ruta de la Reconquista (Etapa 4). You'll also find smaller overlaps with trails like Poncebos - Bulnes - Sotres - Tielve and Vega de Urriellu.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and high-mountain environment, you should bring appropriate mountain biking gear, including a helmet, gloves, and sturdy shoes. Carry enough water and snacks, as well as layers of clothing for changing weather. A basic repair kit for your bike is also highly recommended, as services are limited once you're on the trail.

faqs.question: What are the general regulations for mountain biking within Picos de Europa National Park?
faqs.answer: When mountain biking in Picos de Europa National Park, it's crucial to stay on marked trails to prevent erosion and protect sensitive ecosystems. Respect wildlife, do not disturb livestock, and carry out all your trash. Camping is generally restricted, and fires are prohibited. Always check local signage for any specific rules or temporary closures.
